records.ety: [OE. eve, even, efen, æfen. AS. ǣfen; akin to OS. āband, OFries, āvend, D. avond, OHG. āband, Icel. aptan, Sw. afton, Dan. aften; of unknown origin. Cf. Eve, Evening.]
[AS. efen. efn; akin to OS. eban, D. even, OHG. eban, G. efen, Icel. jafn, Dan. jevn, Sw. jämn, Goth. ibns. Cf. Anent, Ebb.]

defs: Level, smooth, or equal in surface; not rough; free from irregularities; hence uniform in rate of motion of action; as, even ground; an even speed; an even course of conduct.
Equable; not easily ruffled or disturbed; calm; uniformly self-possessed; as, an even temper.
Parallel; on a level; reaching the same limit.
quotes: And shall lay thee even with the ground.

defs: Balanced; adjusted; fair; equitable; impartial; just to both sides; owing nothing on either side; -- said of accounts, bargains, or persons indebted; as, our accounts are even; an even bargain.
quotes: To make the even truth in pleasure flow.

defs: Without an irregularity, flaw, or blemish; pure.
Associate; fellow; of the same condition.
Not odd; capable of division by two without a remainder; -- said of numbers; as, 4 and 10 are even numbers.
quotes: Whether the number of the stars is even or odd.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Evened (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Evening (?)]

defs: To make even or level; to level; to lay smooth.
quotes: His temple Xerxes evened with the soil.

defs: To place in an equal state, as to obligation, or in a state in which nothing is due on either side; to balance, as accounts; to make quits; to make equal; as, to even the score.
To act up to; to keep pace with.

records.ety: [AS. efne. See Even, a., and cf. E'en.]

defs: In an equal or precisely similar manner; equally; precisely; just; likewise; as well.
quotes: Even so did these Gauls possess the coast.

defs: Up to, or down to, an unusual measure or level; so much as; fully; quite.
quotes: Thou wast a soldier Even to Cato's wish.
Without . . . making us even sensible of the change.

defs: As might not be expected; -- serving to introduce what is unexpected or less expected.
quotes: I have made several discoveries, which appear new, even to those who are versed in critical learning.

defs: At the very time; in the very case.
quotes: I knew they were bad enough to please, even when I wrote them.
By these presence, even the presence of Lord Mortimer.
notes: ☞ Even is sometimes used to emphasize a word or phrase. “I have debated even in my soul.”
